summary: The GP of Spa was held under sunny skies. Gasly clinched the victory, starting from P3. An incident-packed race featuring two Safety Cars and a late red flag. The pole sitter lost the lead at the start but fought back through superior race pace and a bold undercut during the second pit window. Pole position was held by Hamilton, and the fastest lap of the race was set by Piastri.

The GP of Bahrain was held under overcast skies. Alonso clinched the victory, starting from P7. Rain started falling on lap 15, creating chaotic track conditions. Teams scrambled to switch to Intermediate and Wet tires. Several drivers spun off, leading to a Virtual Safety Car that decided the race podium. Pole position was held by Leclerc, and the fastest lap of the race was set by Leclerc.

The GP of Spa was held under cloudy skies. Hamilton clinched the victory, starting from P9. A dominant lights-to-flag victory, building a 15-second lead by lap 20 and controlling the race pace. Tire degradation was lower than expected, allowing a comfortable switch to hard tires for the final stint. Pole position was held by Sainz, and the fastest lap of the race was set by Hamilton.

The GP of Singapore was held under rainy skies. Gasly clinched the victory, starting from P1. A highly strategic race characterized by high degradation. The top three finished on different strategies: an aggressive three-stop, a standard two-stop, and a risky one-stop. The two-stop proved victorious. Pole position was held by Piastri, and the fastest lap of the race was set by Sainz.

The GP of Monza was held under cloudy skies. Sainz clinched the victory, starting from P3. A tactical masterclass where the winning driver managed their tires perfectly to execute a one-stop strategy, holding off a late charge from the second-place car on fresher tires. Pole position was held by Hamilton, and the fastest lap of the race was set by Gasly.
